rulings.Case Title: GARY JARUMAY ET AL VS. LILY GROVE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 28, 2018, Line 1 DEFENDANT LILY GROVE'S Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant LILY GROVE'S "Motion for Protective Order and Sanctions" is DENIED. The Subpoena for financial records was issued in the consolidated case in which Defendant LILY GROVES had appeared pursuant to Hamilton v. Asbestos Ltd. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 1127, 1148-1149 and CCP Section 2031.020. The Court finds that Plaintiffs complied with CCP Section 1985.3(1)(b) and proper notice to consumer was attempted on Defendant LILY GROVE. In addition, on or around July 5, 2018, Defendant LILY GROVE received letters from financial institutions providing notice that those records were being sought. The appropriate course of action would have been to bring a Motion to Quash which does not appear in the record. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: GARY JARUMAY ET AL VS. LILY GROVE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 28, 2018, Line 1 DEFENDANT LILY GROVE'S Motion For Relief: Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant LILY GROVE'S "Motion for Relief" is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. On 9/27/28 Plaintiff Mary Ann Flores served Request for Production, Set One by placing the documents under the locked door, during regular business hours at the law office of Denise Wright, counsel for Defendant LILY GROVE. On 10/31/18 responses, containing all objections were served. No extension was obtained by Ms. Wright and the responses were several days late. Defendant LILY GROVE'S motion is GRANTED as to Request for Production No. 15 and her objection is preserved. Federal and state tax returns are privileged and cannot be discovered in litigation, except under very limited circumstances such as voluntary waiver. Webb v. Standard Oil Co. (1957) 49 Cal.2d 509, 512-513; Schnabel v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 704. All other relief in the motion is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: BAY AREA DIAMOND WHOLESALERS, INC. VS. C.H.A CORP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Demand For Production Of Documents, Set One And Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $6,351.00 Plus Costs Against Defendant Chamdi, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, December 28, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF BAY AREA DIAMOND WHOLESALERS, INC. Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Demand For Production Of Documents, Set One And Imposing Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $6,351.00 Plus Costs Against Defendant Chamdi. Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Demand For Production Of Documents, Set One And Imposing Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendants' objections to the following document requests are OVERRULED: 1, 2, 3, 4, 8, 9, 10 (redact unrelated entries in bank statements), 11 (redact unrelated entries in bank statements), 12, 13, 15. Defendant's objections to the following document requests are SUSTAINED: 14, on the grounds articulated in Hecht, Solberg, Robinson, Goldberg & Bagley v. Superior Court, 137 Cal. App. 4th 579, 593-94 (2006) [holding partnerships enjoy some zone of privacy]; H & M Associates v. City of El Centro, 109 Cal. App. 3d 399, 410 (1980) [holding "businesses, regardless of their legal form, have zones of privacy which may not be legitimately invaded"] Sanctions in the amount of $2,500.00 are awarded against Defendant Gideon Chamdi which must be payed to Plaintiff Bay Area Diamond Wholesalers, Inc. within 20 days of entry of the Order on this hearing. Defendants are ORDERED to serve verified written responses to document requests 1, 2, 3, 4, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15 and provide responsive documents within 20 days of entry of the Order on this hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
